title: Frequently Asked Questions on the Phasedown of Hydrofluorocarbons (AIM Act)
claim_supported: Under the AIM Act, EPA is phasing down HFC production/consumption to 15% of baseline (~302.5 MMTEVe consumption / ~382.5 MMTEVe production baselines) by 2036 via step targets (90% of baseline 2022-2023; 60% 2024-2028; 30% 2029-2033; 20% 2034-2035; 15% 2036+); as of 2018, ~75% of US HFC use was in refrigeration/AC equipment; restrictions on higher-GWP HFCs in new refrigeration/AC/heat-pump equipment began January 1, 2025.
locator: epa.gov FAQ page, 'Frequent Questions on the Phasedown of Hydrofluorocarbons,' phasedown schedule and refrigeration-sector-share sections
notes: Directly fetched this session. Directly load-bearing for any idea premised on refrigerant transition timing for two-phase/immersion cooling fluids or vapor-compression cooling equipment in the US.

authors_or_org: National Standardization Administration of China (国家标准委) / TC20
claim_supported: Current GB 40879 national standard sets data-center PUE limit value at 1.5, Grade-2 at 1.3, and Grade-1 at 1.2; a 2025 revision (public-comment period March 17-April 16, 2025) proposes tightening new/expanded large and super-large data-center PUE to <=1.25 by end of 2025, and national-hub-node data centers to <=1.2, with the revision also proposing to remove the current standard's scope limitation to air-cooled/electric-chiller facilities so it applies regardless of cooling method.

notes: Directly fetched this session, official Chinese national standards database (SAMR). Chinese-language government/standard primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota; load-bearing PUE numbers are quoted directly from the fetched page.

title: Launch of second Data Centre - Call for Application (DC-CFA2)
authors_or_org: Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB) and Infocomm Media Development Authority (IMDA)
claim_supported: EDB and IMDA launched DC-CFA2 on December 1, 2025, allocating at least 200 MW of new data-center capacity (application window closed March 31, 2026); operators must be at least 50% powered via eligible green pathways, obtain BCA-IMDA Green Mark for Data Centres 2024 Platinum certification, achieve PUE <=1.25 at 100% IT load, and comply with Singapore Standard SS 715:2025 on energy efficiency of data-centre IT equipment. title: Project Deschutes: A 2MW Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU) specification
authors_or_org: Google (presented via ARPA-E workshop; published through Open Compute Project)
claim_supported: Google published (and contributed to OCP) the Project Deschutes 2 MW coolant-distribution-unit (CDU) specification, requiring an aggressive 3C approach temperature difference (ATD), ~80 PSI available pressure, fully redundant per-pump-circuit power feeds, and 0.2-micron side-stream filtration; multiple cooling vendors (Boyd, CoolerMaster, Delta, Envicool, Nidec, nVent, Vertiv) have since built CDUs to this open specification.
locator: ARPA-E-hosted workshop slide deck, 'Project Deschutes: A 2MW CDU' (Google, Kabbani), Jan. 2026 workshop
notes: WebFetch retrieved the PDF binary but could not extract readable text this session; the technical figures above (3C ATD, 80 PSI, 0.2-micron filtration) are drawn from search-result summaries of vendor/trade coverage (Boyd, DCD, HPCwire) of the same specification, not independently re-derived from the raw slide text. This is a hyperscaler-originated buyer specification now functioning as a quasi-industry-standard -- a strong US buyer/procurement demand signal.

title: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack-scale liquid-cooled system
claim_supported: NVIDIA states the GB200 NVL72 rack-scale system (36 Grace CPUs + 72 Blackwell GPUs, 13.4 TB HBM3E, 130 TB/s NVLink bandwidth) uses a rack-scale liquid-cooled design mandatory at this power density, claiming 25x more performance at the same power vs. H100 air-cooled infrastructure and reduced water consumption; third-party technical commentary (not NVIDIA's own page) estimates ~72 kW of GPU heat alone per rack (1000W/GPU x 72) and >120 kW total rack heat, with recommended cold-plate flow of 2-3 L/min per module and manifold-level flow exceeding 700 LPM.
locator: nvidia.com GB200 NVL72 product page
notes: Directly fetched this session. Precise per-GPU TDP (1000-1200W depending on source) and rack-level flow/pressure figures are third-party technical-press estimates (Introl, ToneCooling, SemiAnalysis, Alliance Chemical), not NVIDIA's own disclosed spec sheet numbers -- treat as vendor performance claim (T2) rather than an independently audited figure; flag the TDP discrepancy (1000W vs. 1200W per GPU across sources) as unresolved.

title: Flex Acquires JetCool to Expand Data Center and Power Portfolio
claim_supported: Flex (NASDAQ: FLEX) announced November 14, 2024 acquisition of JetCool Technologies (founded 2019, Littleton MA), a microconvective direct-to-chip liquid-cooling company; deal financial terms not disclosed. JetCool's CDU handles racks up to 300 kW, scalable to over 2 MW in row-based configurations, following an OCP Global Summit 2024 partnership announcement.
locator: investors.flex.com official press release, dated November 14, 2024
notes: Directly fetched via Flex's own investor-relations press release page this session. Deal value not disclosed by either party; do not infer a dollar figure. JetCool separately received a $1,265,747 DOE COOLERCHIPS subcontract in 2023 (see L14-030), a useful cross-link between government R&D funding and subsequent private M&A.

title: ZutaCore Secures $100M+ Series C Funding to Accelerate AI Data Center Cooling Innovation
claim_supported: ZutaCore raised $100M+ Series C funding (dated June 2, 2026) from investors including Mitsubishi Electric, Carrier Ventures, and Samsung Electronics (via Samsung Ventures), to scale its waterless, direct-to-chip, two-phase liquid-cooling platform (aimed at processors exceeding 4,000 W); company states 75+ global deployments and a 2 MW emulation platform for validation at scale.
locator: blog.zutacore.com press release, dated June 2, 2026
notes: Directly fetched this session from the company's own press-release blog (not an independent financial-press or SEC-type filing, hence T2 rather than T1). Strategic investors (Mitsubishi Electric, Samsung Electronics) are themselves major electronics/cooling-adjacent companies -- a useful corporate-strategic-interest signal for the Asia section of the brief.

title: Schneider Electric Unveils Liquid Cooling Portfolio with Motivair
claim_supported: Schneider Electric (which acquired Motivair in February 2025) unveiled an integrated liquid-cooling portfolio combining Schneider infrastructure/software/services with Motivair's CDU technology (scaling 105 kW to 2.5 MW, certified for NVIDIA's latest hardware, used in 6 of the world's top-10 supercomputers), targeting rack densities beyond 140 kW today and up to 1 MW in the future.
locator: motivaircorp.com press coverage of the Schneider Electric/Motivair liquid-cooling portfolio launch, 2025
notes: Direct fetch of se.com's own press release returned HTTP 403 this session; figures instead drawn from motivaircorp.com's own reporting of the same announcement plus ARC Advisory Group secondary coverage. Motivair acquisition closing date (Feb. 2025) and deal value not independently confirmed this session (deal value not found in any source reviewed).

title: Vertiv Holdings Co. FY2025 liquid-cooling order growth and backlog (10-K / investor disclosures)
claim_supported: Vertiv reported FY2025 total revenue of $10.2 billion, management-stated liquid-cooling order growth of 50%+ in 2025, a Q4 2025 book-to-bill ratio of 2.9, and an order backlog of $15 billion entering 2026; company targets $13.25-13.75B revenue for 2026.
locator: SEC EDGAR CIK 1674101, Form 10-K filed Feb. 13, 2026 (vrt-20251231.htm); figures cross-reported via Seeking Alpha and Motley Fool investor commentary
notes: Direct WebFetch of the SEC.gov 10-K URL returned HTTP 403 this session (consistent with SEC EDGAR bot-blocking seen in other lanes of this project); figures above are drawn from investor-press secondary aggregation of Vertiv's own reported/quoted figures, not independently re-derived from the primary filing text. Verifier should fetch the canonical 10-K directly (e.g., via an authenticated EDGAR tool) to confirm exact segment-level liquid-cooling revenue figures before treating any specific dollar amount as fully confirmed.

title: GS칼텍스, 데이터센터용 직접액체냉각유체 출시 (GS Caltex launches direct liquid cooling fluid for data centers)
claim_supported: GS Caltex launched 'Kixx DLC Fluid PG25' (propylene-glycol-based, organic-acid-technology corrosion inhibitors) for direct-liquid-cooling of CPUs/GPUs on October 14, 2025; company previously supplied immersion-cooling fluid to Samsung SDS (2024) and demonstrated at LG U+'s Pyeongchon data center (2025), and partnered with Supermicro for AI-server thermal testing at its Daejeon research facility; a September 2025 agreement with GS Engineering & Construction and SDT targets immersion-cooling-system development and validation.
locator: gscaltexmediahub.com official company press release, dated Oct. 14, 2025
notes: Directly fetched this session from GS Caltex's own media hub (company-primary source). Korean-language local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota; names real named-customer relationships (Samsung SDS, LG U+, Supermicro) making it a useful demand-evidence record for the Korea section.

title: 生成AI時代のデータセンター空調技術を結集させた検証施設 (Verification facility integrating generative-AI-era data center cooling technologies)
claim_supported: NTT Facilities opened its 'Products Engineering Hub for Data Center Cooling' (DC Cooling Hub) within the NTT Musashino R&D Center, fully operational April 22, 2025, as a verification/showroom facility for cooling equipment, with simulated mock loads totaling 780 kW (420 kW air-cooled, 360 kW liquid-cooled), testing direct liquid cooling (CDU + stainless piping), chiller-less hybrid dry-cooler systems, and oil-free chillers.
locator: ntt-f.co.jp official news release, dated April 15, 2025 (facility operational from April 22, 2025)
notes: Directly fetched this session from NTT Facilities' own Japanese-language news page (company-primary source). Japanese-language local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota. A separate, related NTT initiative (a Data Center Trial Field in Chiba, opened Nov. 2024) and a Mitsubishi Heavy Industries/NTT Communications/NEC two-phase direct-chip-cooling demonstration were found via search but not independently fetched this session -- follow-up candidates for a future scout wave.

title: LiquidStack acquired by Trane Technologies
claim_supported: Trane Technologies (publicly traded HVAC major) acquired two-phase-immersion-cooling vendor LiquidStack on February 10, 2026; LiquidStack's last disclosed private funding round was $20M in September 2024 prior to the acquisition.
locator: Tracxn company-profile aggregator page, LiquidStack funding/M&A history
notes: Sourced from an investment-data aggregator (Tracxn), not directly from Trane Technologies' or LiquidStack's own press release this session -- classified as trade_press/aggregator rather than a directly fetched company filing. Verifier should locate and fetch Trane Technologies' own M&A press release or 8-K disclosure to upgrade this to a directly confirmed company filing.

title: Industry Consultation Meeting of National Data Centre Policy 2025
authors_or_org: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India
claim_supported: MeitY (chaired by its Secretary) held an industry consultation meeting on India's draft National Data Centre Policy 2025 on August 5, 2025 at Vigyan Bhawan, New Delhi, with industry associations, data-centre developers, cloud service providers, and government departments, to refine the draft policy; separate trade reporting (not independently confirmed in this fetched page) describes the draft policy's stated ambition of 2,000 MW of new data-centre capacity by 2027 and an emphasis on energy-efficient/sustainable/green data-centre design.
locator: digitalindia.gov.in official photo-gallery/event page, dated August 5, 2025 event
notes: Directly fetched this session from India's official Digital India government portal. The 2,000 MW/2027 capacity figure and specific incentive details are from secondary trade-press reporting (w.media) not corroborated in the fetched government page itself -- treat those specific figures as unconfirmed pending a verifier locating the actual draft-policy PDF text. This is the lane's sole India-geography record; a future scout wave should prioritize finding the primary draft-policy document and any India-specific liquid-cooling/thermal mandate.

title: ASHRAE Datacom Series Book 4: Liquid Cooling Guidelines for Datacom Equipment Centers, 2nd Edition
authors_or_org: ASHRAE (American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers) / TC 9.9
claim_supported: ASHRAE's Datacom Series Book 4 (2nd Edition), 'Liquid Cooling Guidelines for Datacom Equipment Centers,' is the industry-reference standard-adjacent guideline covering interface requirements between chilled-water/facility-water systems and technology cooling systems, approach temperatures, liquid immersion cooling, and wetted-material/water-quality requirements; ASHRAE's broader TC 9.9 content migrated in 2024 into a paid-subscription 'Datacom Encyclopedia' (list price $33/year, $24/year for ASHRAE members) that includes PDFs of all 14 books in the historical Datacom Series.
locator: webstore.ansi.org product listing page for the ASHRAE Datacom Book 4, 2nd Edition; Datacom Encyclopedia pricing from ashrae.org bookstore search result
notes: Direct WebFetch of the ANSI webstore page returned HTTP 403 this session; edition/title/content-scope details and Encyclopedia pricing are drawn from search-result snippets (ANSI webstore listing plus ashrae.org bookstore description), not independently confirmed by direct fetch. Exact 2nd-edition publication year approximate (search snippets did not give an unambiguous single year); verifier should confirm directly.

title: 工業技術與資訊》迎戰2,400瓦的散熱天塹 低壓冷媒兩相流冷板 揭開AI頂級算力封印 (Facing the 2,400W thermal barrier: low-pressure-refrigerant two-phase-flow cold plate unlocks top-tier AI compute)
authors_or_org: Industrial Technology Research Institute (工研院, ITRI), reported by Liberty Times (自由時報/自由評論網)
claim_supported: Taiwan's Industrial Technology Research Institute (ITRI) announced (article dated July 11, 2026) a low-pressure-refrigerant two-phase-flow cold-plate technology achieving single-chip heat-dissipation capacity of 2,400 W, with phase-change heat removal 5-10x that of traditional single-phase liquid cooling and over 40% energy savings versus conventional liquid cooling at equivalent dissipation; ITRI has begun partnering with domestic cooling-supply-chain companies across cold-plate manufacturing, CDUs, components/interface materials, and system testing to build a complete two-phase cold-plate industrial chain.
locator: talk.ltn.com.tw (自由評論論壇/自由時報), article dated 2026-07-11 17:00
notes: Directly fetched this session; Traditional-Chinese-language Taiwan local-primary source satisfying Asia local-language quota. This is trade-press reporting of an ITRI (a government-affiliated, non-profit R&D institute akin to a national lab in Taiwan) announcement rather than ITRI's own primary release -- verifier should attempt to locate ITRI's own press release or technical paper to upgrade tier/source_type. Publication date (July 11, 2026) is one day before this scouting session, making this the most current record in the lane.

title: Data Center Liquid Cooling Market Size, Share & Trends Analysis Report
claim_supported: Grand View Research estimates the global data-center liquid-cooling market at $6.65 billion in 2025, projected to reach $29.46 billion by 2033 (CAGR ~20.1%, 2026-2033); other consultancy estimates found this session for the same nominal market range widely, from $3.42B (2022, Virtue Market Research) to $5.38B (2024, GlobeNewswire/ResearchAndMarkets) to $4.8B (2025, Global Market Insights) with projected 2030/2033/2035 endpoints spanning $15.26B to $27.1B to $29.46B depending on the firm and methodology.
locator: grandviewresearch.com market-report landing page summary
notes: Consultancy market-size estimate, not independently fetched in full this session (landing-page summary only) and not bottom-up-verified; per SOURCE_STANDARDS, vendor/consultancy market sizing requires bottom-up arithmetic plus independent triangulation before use as a load-bearing TAM figure -- the wide spread across five different research firms found this session (documented in the notes) should be treated as an explicit unresolved contradiction, not averaged into a single number, in any downstream market-sizing work.
